overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions and voted to forward a modified fee waiver resolution to the full county board to assist tornado victims, limiting the waiver to septic system reconnect fees only. The committee also received educational presentations from Solid Waste, Parks Recreation and Forestry, and Conservation Planning and Zoning departments.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public hearing: Tim Veland rezoning petition (Urban Residential/General Agriculture to Rural Residential, Town of Green Valley)
Public hearing: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture, Town of McMillan)
Consideration of fee waivers for properties impacted by April 17, 2026 severe weather
Solid Waste Department presentation by Dave Hagenbusher
Parks, Recreation and Forestry Department presentation by Tom Lovelin

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made a motion to approve the minutes, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. The motion carried unanimously with no discussion.

discussions.item: Tim Veland rezoning petition (Town of Green Valley)
discussions.body: Land Use Specialist Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning from Urban Residential and General Agriculture to Rural Residential for a proposed land division. The Town of Green Valley recommended approval on March 10, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact, conclusions of law, and town approval. Supervisor Kurt seconded. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (Town of McMillan)
discussions.body: Garrett Pogle presented the petition to rezone from Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature, and Pogle explained the property contains a home and horse riding stable, with the rezoning needed to make the home a conforming use. The Town of McMillan recommended approval on April 20, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Sefeld moved to approve, Supervisor Drew seconded. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Fee waivers for April 17, 2026 severe weather victims
discussions.body: Director Lori Mskimmons presented a proposal to waive county zoning and septic fees for properties damaged by the April 17 tornado affecting Ringle, Kronenwetter, Easton, and Weston. Significant debate ensued. Supervisor Drew questioned fairness to future individual disaster victims not covered by emergency declarations. Supervisor Leur asked about levy impacts. Supervisor Drebeck expressed concern about setting precedent and proposed an amendment limiting waivers to reconnect fees only ($160 each). The amendment passed, and the amended resolution was approved to forward to the county board, though not unanimously.

Director Dave Hagenbusher provided an overview of landfill operations, explaining the facility accepts about 1,000 tons daily with 11 FTEs. He highlighted the renewable natural gas partnership with Vidi Energy generating revenue, significant capital expenses of $4-6 million for expansions and closures, and new state requirements for leachate treatment by 2032. Marilyn Bend praised the department for helping Ringle tornado victims and keeping private landfill costs competitive.

discussions.item: Parks, Recreation and Forestry Department presentation
discussions.body: Tom Lovelin, County Forest Administrator, presented on behalf of Director Jamie who had an emergency. He explained the department operates both county and City of Wausau park systems with 44 full-time and 120 seasonal employees. A Nine Mile County Forest study showed out-of-town visitors generate $3.25 million annually for Marathon County's economy. Supervisor Sefeld noted this was Lovelin's last committee meeting before his June 1st retirement after nearly 30 years of service.

actionItems: Rezoning petition for James Offer and David Tract (Urban Residential/General Agriculture to Rural Residential in Town of Green Valley) approved and forwarded to County Board
Rezoning petition for Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz (Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture in Town of McMillan) approved and forwarded to County Board
Modified fee waiver resolution limiting waivers to septic reconnect fees only for April 17, 2026 tornado victims forwarded to County Board for consideration
Track costs and requests related to fee waivers for future policy development
CPZ to present conservation topics at future summer educational meeting
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
